name: Mardi Gras
description: Beads, krewes, king cake, and the last big party before Lent.

questions.q: What does 'Mardi Gras' literally translate to in English?

source.notes: Refers to the last day of indulgent eating before the fasting season of Lent.

questions.q: Which U.S. city claims the country's oldest Mardi Gras celebration?

source.notes: Mobile celebrated Mardi Gras decades before New Orleans was founded — earliest celebration documented in 1703.

questions.q: What are the three traditional colors of Mardi Gras?

source.notes: Declared official by the Rex krewe in 1872.

questions.q: What do the three Mardi Gras colors traditionally represent?

source.notes: Purple = justice, Green = faith, Gold = power.

questions.q: What is the most-quoted Cajun French phrase associated with New Orleans Mardi Gras?
What pastry is the traditional sweet served throughout Mardi Gras / Carnival season?

source.notes: Decorated with the three official colors.

questions.q: King cakes are named for which biblical event celebrated on Epiphany (Jan 6)?
choices: The Visit of the Three Wise Men (Magi)

source.original_a: The story of the Three Wise Men
source.notes: Carnival season starts on the Feast of the Epiphany and ends on Mardi Gras.

questions.q: What is hidden inside a New Orleans king cake — and obligates whoever finds it to provide next year's cake?

source.notes: The 'baby' (often plastic Jesus figurine) is baked in or hidden under after baking. Whoever gets the slice with the baby buys the next cake — and may host the next king cake party.

questions.q: What do Louisiana state law and city ordinance require Mardi Gras float riders to wear?

source.notes: Originally for class-blurring anonymity; codified into law in the 20th century.

questions.q: What do parade-goers shout to encourage float riders to throw beads?
Which prized New Orleans throw is given out only by an all-Black krewe with a 100+ year history?

source.original_a: Zulu coconuts, Muses shoes, and Krewe of Iris sunglasses
source.notes: Krewe of Zulu Social Aid and Pleasure Club, founded 1909. Each coconut is hand-decorated.

questions.q: What is the term used in New Orleans for the parading mystic societies?

source.notes: From 'crew', spelled with the Old English style.

questions.q: Which krewe held the first formal Mardi Gras parade in New Orleans in 1857?

source.original_a: The Mistick Krewe of Comus in 1853
source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: rephrased — Comus was founded in 1856 and held its first parade Feb 24, 1857. Named for the Greek god of revelry.

questions.q: Which Texas city has held a major Mardi Gras celebration since 1867?

source.notes: Galveston, on the Gulf coast, hosts one of the largest U.S. Mardi Gras after New Orleans and Mobile.

questions.q: Mardi Gras is followed the next day by which Christian observance, marking the start of Lent?

source.notes: Lent is 40 days of fasting before Easter.

questions.q: What is the Italian name for Mardi Gras / Carnival's last day?

source.notes: Literally 'Fat Tuesday' in Italian. The famous Venetian Carnival is 'Carnevale di Venezia'.

questions.q: What is the German term for the carnival/Mardi Gras tradition celebrated in cities like Cologne?

source.notes: Cologne's 'Kölner Karneval' is one of the largest in Europe. Other regional names include Fasching (Bavaria) and Fastnacht (Switzerland).

questions.q: Which Belgian Carnival's parade — featuring 'Gilles' who throw oranges — is on UNESCO's intangible cultural heritage list?
Who reigns as 'King of Carnival' on Mardi Gras Day in New Orleans, traditionally receiving the city key from the mayor?

source.original_a: He is chosen by the Rex Organization and represents the Carnival king

questions.q: How does the New Orleans Police Department traditionally signal the official end of Carnival at midnight?

source.original_a: Police officers moving through crowds on Bourbon Street at midnight
source.notes: On horseback, sweeping revelers off the street to begin Ash Wednesday.
